conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do have a child. I have one child who is 8 years old and lives with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask:

Are you currently responsible for this child, meaning you're the one who primarily looks after them and pays for things like their food, clothes, and other needs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm the one who looks after them and pay for everything they need - their food, clothes, school things, all of that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Now I need to ask about your immigration status:

Do you live in the UK, and are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or another type of immigration status that allows you to access public funds?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and I'm able to access public funds. I'm settled here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have just one more question:

Is your child currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training, or are they under 16 years old?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're only 8 years old, so they're under 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit** for your 8-year-old child.

You meet all the key eligibility requirements:
- You're responsible for a child under 16 years old
- You live with your child in the UK
- You look after them and pay for their needs (food, clothes, school items, etc.)
- You're settled in the UK with access to public funds

You can proceed with applying for Child Benefit for your child. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - child_0 is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding child_0:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: The child is 8 years old, which means they are under 16. The claimant lives with the child in the UK, is responsible for the child (looking after them and paying for their needs such as food, clothes, and school items), and has settled status in the UK with access to public funds. According to the eligibility criteria, you can get Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training) and you live in the UK. All of these conditions are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the key eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: they are responsible for an 8-year-old child who lives with them in the UK, they pay for the child's needs, and they are settled in the UK with access to public funds. The child is under 16 years old, which satisfies the age requirement.
